purpose: Prevent free-form rule labels from licensing unsupported ordinal mechanism-preservation scores.
epistemic_boundary: These rules govern evidence-to-score transformations only. They do not establish consciousness, nonconsciousness, sentience, personhood, identity, moral status, or global theory truth.

rules.rationale: A maximum mapping score requires an explicit pre-outcome graph, component mapping, bounded mismatches, and declared intervention predictions. Terminology or architectural resemblance is insufficient.
A maximum selective-intervention score requires replicated effects in the preregistered direction that survive selectivity and control checks.
A score of two is licensed when state substitution changes the predicted outcome with partial specificity, without claiming recovery of the full dependency matrix.
A score of two requires failure of the theater account on hidden-state-linked measures, while reserving score three for a preregistered cross-domain mechanistic battery.
A score of two requires complete reporting and passing of the protocol matching battery, but does not claim exhaustive exclusion of every generic-capacity explanation.
A maximum measurement score requires adequate measurement under blinding, multiple probes, multiple prompts, and report suppression.
